title: Alert without for duration — flapping on transient spikes
code_snippet:
- alert: HighP99Latency
  expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(http_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le)) > 2
  labels:
    severity: warning
  annotations:
    summary: 'p99 latency above 2s'
expected_feedback: Standard: Alert has no 'for' duration clause. A momentary latency spike (single slow request, GC pause) will trigger and immediately resolve the alert — generating a page notification that's already stale by the time on-call reads it. This is a primary cause of alert fatigue. Add 'for: 5m' for warning severity to require sustained condition before firing. For critical severity, use 'for: 2m' to balance speed vs noise.
violated_rule: for duration set on all alerts to prevent flapping
